role: Speaker of Parliament (2004–2008); Head of Iranian Academy of Persian Language and Literature
bio: Gholam-Ali Haddad-Adel is a philosopher and politician who served as Speaker of Parliament from 2004 to 2008, the first non-cleric to hold the role since the 1979 revolution. He holds a PhD in philosophy from the University of Tehran and has led the Iranian [Academy of Persian Language and Literature](/orgs/iranian-academy-of-persian-language) since 1995. His daughter Zahra married [Mojtaba Khamenei](/people/mojtaba-khamenei), son of the Supreme Leader, making Haddad-Adel one of the most personally connected figures to the supreme leadership. He is a senior member of the principlist [Alliance of Builders of Islamic Iran](/orgs/alliance-of-builders-of-islamic-iran).
editorial_notes: Father-in-law of Mojtaba Khamenei (son of Supreme Leader). First non-clerical Majlis Speaker in IRI history. Adviser to Supreme Leader since 2008.
